description: Honduran criminal organization dedicated to drug trafficking, founded in the early 1990s by cattle ranchers in Colón and Olancho departments. It became one of the largest transport groups in Honduras, accumulating a net worth of nearly one billion dollars. The group purchased cocaine from Colombian organizations and passed it to the Sinaloa Cartel and other Mexican groups. It had extensive business and political interests, including ties to the Honduran elite and government officials.

country_links.evidence_quote: Se cree que el grupo compraba las drogas a organizaciones colombianas
country_links.context: Los Cachiros bought drugs from Colombian organizations.

country_links.evidence_quote: Luego pasaban la cocaína al Cartel de Sinaloa y a otros grupos mexicanos
country_links.context: Los Cachiros passed cocaine to Mexican groups including the Sinaloa Cartel.

country_links.evidence_quote: Se cree que el grupo compraba las drogas a organizaciones colombianas, posiblemente en Nicaragua
country_links.context: The organization buys drugs from Colombian organizations possibly in Nicaragua.

country_links.evidence_quote: se entregó al gobierno de Estados Unidos en enero del 2015
country_links.context: A leader of Los Cachiros surrendered to the US government and was prosecuted there.
